title: Gooey Grilled Turkish Bread Flatbread
description: A malty, chewy, and cozy Turkish dish featuring bread flour, perfect for a diabetic-friendly snack, served in a molcajete vessel.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine bread flour, instant yeast, and salt.
Gradually add olive oil and water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased molcajete vessel, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a grill to medium heat and grill the dough for 2-3 minutes on each side, until it is golden brown and cooked through.
If using malted milk powder, brush it onto the bread during the last minute of grilling.
Remove the bread from the grill and sprinkle with sesame seeds if desired.
Let the bread cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Serve the gooey grilled Turkish bread flatbread warm, straight from the molcajete vessel.
